entries.text: Open, Save, and Save As for .rms files.
App settings, including how these help popups behave.
The name of the currently open map, taken from its filename. Shows "Untitled Map" until you open or save a file.
When this map was last saved during this session. Shows a dash until you save at least once.
Shows your script as editable command blocks instead of raw code — good for building without memorizing syntax. Arrives in Phase 3.
The full code editor, with RMS syntax highlighting and hover docs.
A pane for built-in and (later) community-made tools that operate on your script. Arrives in Phase 5.
Total resources across the whole map, summed from every create_object placement the parser can see. Shown as a min-max range when if/start_random blocks make the exact count generation-dependent.

Resources a single player gets from set_place_for_every_player placements — per player, not multiplied by player count (see Total for the map-wide sum across every player). v1 doesn't yet correlate placement with a specific player's land.
Resources placed without set_place_for_every_player — everything not counted as Player. v1 doesn't yet check whether these sit inside or outside player territory.
Errors and warnings found by the parser in the current file, re-checked live as you type on the Code tab. Click a squiggle in the editor to see the full message.
Opens Generation Settings — map size and player count, used to compute the resource totals shown here.
How many players this map is generated for. Scales resources placed with set_place_for_every_player in the totals above.
The map size this script targets. Not yet used by the resource totals — will feed the approximate preview and consistency checker in a later phase.
Controls when these help popups appear: always on hover, only while holding ALT, or off entirely.
